title: Saltzman: Space ‘baked into’ modern combat operations
summary: Space Force personnel are actively supporting combat operations under U.S. Central Command from multiple locations, including the Middle East, Tampa, and South Carolina. General Saltzman emphasized the critical role of space in modern military operations.

title: Aspect Aerospace secures early funding to advance swarm-deployable VLEO satellites
summary: Aspect Aerospace, a University of South Alabama spin-off, has secured $2.4 million in early funding to develop small, swarm-deployable spacecraft for very low Earth orbit (VLEO) missions. These circuit-board-sized satellites aim to monitor the space environment.

title: Creating near-term lunar settlements: lessons from space history
summary: The article reflects on space history milestones, including Robert Goddard's first liquid-fueled rocket flight in 1926 and Sputnik's launch in 1957, to draw lessons for near-term lunar settlements. It highlights the rapid progress in space exploration over the past century.

title: Artemis 2 fueling underway
summary: Fueling of the Space Launch System (SLS) for Artemis 2 is underway, with a planned April 1 launch attempt. This mission will mark the first human return to the moon in over 50 years.

title: From the Midwest to the Moon
summary: Ohio is emerging as a key hub for space companies, attracting investments and partnerships for lunar missions. The state's growing aerospace infrastructure and skilled workforce are driving its role in the space industry.

title: The Florida Model for Sustainable Aerospace Growth
summary: Space Florida's President and CEO, Robert Long, discusses the Florida Model for sustainable aerospace growth. The conversation highlights strategies for fostering long-term development in the aerospace sector.

title: After three years, Artemis 2 astronauts ready to launch
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts, who have been in the public eye for three years, are now ready for their upcoming launch. Despite their long preparation, surprises may still arise as they near the mission.

title: Vantor wins intelligence agency contract to monitor space objects
summary: Vantor has secured a contract under the NGA's Luno program to monitor space objects in low Earth orbit and provide insights on priority targets. The deal highlights Vantor's role in supporting intelligence agency efforts in space situational awareness.

title: Chinese startup tests flexible robotic arm in space for on-orbit servicing
summary: A Chinese commercial company successfully tested a flexible robotic arm in space for on-orbit servicing, including satellite servicing, refueling, and debris removal. This demonstration highlights advancements in China's commercial space capabilities.
company: Chinese commercial company (unnamed in the article)

title: Virgin Galactic expects commercial suborbital flights to resume late this year
summary: Virgin Galactic plans to resume commercial suborbital flights by the end of the year as it nears completion of its next-generation spaceplane. The company aims to restart operations after a pause in its commercial services.

title: Watch Artemis 2 fly through space in real time with this telescope livestream
summary: The Virtual Telescope Project will livestream NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft in real time after its launch. Viewers can watch the spacecraft travel through space via the livestream.

title: April's full 'Pink Moon' rises tonight — just as NASA readies its Artemis 2 moon mission
summary: The full 'Pink Moon' will rise tonight, coinciding with NASA's upcoming Artemis 2 mission launch, which will send a crew of four to lunar orbit.

title: Satellite spies SpaceX Starlink before it breaks apart | Space photo of the day for April 1, 2026
summary: A satellite captured an image of a SpaceX Starlink spacecraft before it disintegrated in orbit. This event highlights ongoing concerns about space debris and satellite longevity.

title: Saturn's magnetic field is curiously warped, and one of its moons may be to blame
summary: Saturn's magnetic field is unusually warped, potentially due to one of its moons. This discovery comes as plans for future missions to Saturn and its moon Enceladus are being developed.

title: NASA set to launch Artemis 2 moon mission today, the 1st crewed lunar flight since 1972
summary: NASA is set to launch the Artemis 2 moon mission today, marking the first crewed lunar flight since 1972. The mission aims to continue human exploration of the moon after decades.

title: This star-forming galaxy is blowing out powerful winds topping 2 million mph
summary: NASA's XRISM X-ray spacecraft measured winds of 2 million mph emanating from a distant star-forming galaxy. These powerful outflows are being driven by intense star formation activity within the galaxy.

title: Yes, NASA's launching Artemis 2 astronauts to the moon on April Fools' Day. It's not a joke.
summary: NASA is scheduled to launch the Artemis 2 mission to the moon on April Fools' Day, aiming for a historic lunar mission rather than a joke.

title: Comet MAPS faces a make-or-break moment as it dives toward the sun on April 4 — could it shine in the daytime sky?
summary: Comet MAPS is approaching its closest encounter with the sun on April 4, passing just 101,000 miles above the solar surface. Its survival could determine if it becomes visible in the daytime sky.

title: How to use your Unistellar smart telescope to track the Artemis 2 rocket light curve in the name of science
summary: Citizen scientists are using Unistellar smart telescopes to track the Artemis 2 rocket's light curve for scientific purposes. Unistellar's co-founder discussed this engagement in planetary defense with Space.com.

title: What will happen if Artemis 2 astronauts get hit by a solar storm during NASA's ambitious moon mission?
summary: NASA is preparing contingency plans for Artemis 2 astronauts in case they encounter a severe solar storm during their moon mission. The crew can take shelter in specific areas of the Orion capsule, such as storage bays or near the toilet, to minimize radiation exposure.

title: Artemis II set to return humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: Artemis II is scheduled to launch on Wednesday evening to return humans to the Moon, marking a significant milestone in space exploration. The mission follows the historic Apollo 17 landing in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz from locations in Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights advancements in space exploration and rocket technology.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California for the Transporter 16 mission. This marks another successful rideshare mission for the company.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA revealed detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned to proceed with the launch but faced an issue that led to the cancellation.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. The initiative was revealed by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other NASA leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to Starbase launch pads in preparation for the Block 3 version of Starship. This effort highlights SpaceX's focus on increasing launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the United States will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows repairs made to the launch site after the previous Soyuz MS-28 launch.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting industry events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator (LSS) chamber. The mission is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket by Arianespace in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will launch ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use the new costing software in their financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky traditional antennas.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the root cause and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates microgravity conditions for testing space-related technologies. This initiative aims to provide hands-on experience and foster innovation among the next generation of space scientists.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding. The company aims to build an uncrewed space station to host manufacturing infrastructure in orbit.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has raised $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with participation from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker has expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base as a stepping stone for future Mars missions. The remarks highlight growing enthusiasm for NASA's Artemis program and lunar exploration efforts.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager and Icarus are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), highlighting advanced space technology integration. This partnership marks a significant step in ISS operations, blending futuristic concepts with real-world applications.

title: Q&A With Lunar Base Manager Carlos Garcia-Galan
summary: Carlos Garcia-Galan transitioned from managing NASA’s lunar-orbiting habitat program to overseeing the agency’s rapid lunar surface base initiative. He discussed these plans during NASA’s Ignition event.

title: Transporter-16 Sends 119 Payloads to Orbit
summary: The Transporter-16 mission successfully launched 119 payloads into orbit. This marks another 'rideshare' mission delivering multiple small satellites and payloads for various customers.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M Series A at $1.1B Valuation
summary: Starcloud has raised $170 million in a Series A funding round, valuing the company at $1.1 billion. This investment underscores strong investor interest in orbital data center ventures.

title: Bellatrix Raises $20M For In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million for developing in-space propulsion technology. The company aims to scale production after flight-qualifying its core technologies.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS network. The company aims to provide stronger, more accurate, and secure PNT signals in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: Jared Isaacman, NASA Administrator, marks 100 days in office by emphasizing a renewed focus on lunar missions. The statement signals an intensified push toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: Faced With Starlink Competition, Ground Segment Leaders Turn to Orchestration, Specialization
summary: Ground segment providers are adapting to competition from LEO megaconstellations like Starlink by focusing on orchestration and specialization. This shift aims to enhance efficiency and differentiate their offerings in the evolving space industry.

title: Delta Taps Amazon Leo for IFC Rollout on 500 Aircraft
summary: Delta Air Lines has partnered with Amazon's Leo project to deploy in-flight connectivity (IFC) services on 500 aircraft starting in 2028. This collaboration marks a significant expansion of Delta's connectivity offerings using Amazon's satellite-based technology.

title: Air Force Awards BlackSky $99M to Large Aperture Optical Payload for Space-Based Imaging
summary: The U.S. Air Force awarded BlackSky a $99 million contract to accelerate the design and development of a large aperture optical payload for space-based imaging. The contract was received from the Air Force Research Laboratory in early March.

title: Claussen Departs, ST Engineering iDirect Names Sridhar Kuppanna as CEO
summary: ST Engineering iDirect has appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as CEO, succeeding Don Claussen who served in the role for approximately three years.

title: Es’hailSat and EgyptSat Sign VSAT Partnership Deal
summary: Es’hailSat and EgyptSat have signed a multi-year VSAT partnership deal to enhance connectivity services in the Middle East. The agreement aims to improve regional satellite communication capabilities.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M to Fund Orbital Data Center Plans
summary: Starcloud, a space startup, raised $170 million in a Series A funding round to develop orbital data centers using satellites. The company is now valued at $1.1 billion.

title: WAVE Achieves First Cloud-to-Gateway Satcom Virtualization with AI Signal Analysis
summary: The WAVE Consortium, an IEEE standards body, achieved the first standardized waveform virtualization using FPGA acceleration for cloud-to-gateway satellite communications (Satcom) with AI signal analysis.

title: SpaceX Launches 119 Payloads on the Transporter-16 Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads into Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) on the Transporter-16 rideshare mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base. This dedicated smallsat mission marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: Live in the Booth: AST SpaceMobile President Scott Wisniewski Talks Spectrum Strategy and Defense Potential
summary: AST SpaceMobile President Scott Wisniewski discusses the company's spectrum strategy and defense potential during SATShow Week, highlighting the recent launch of the BlueBird 6 satellite.

title: SpaceBridge Launches UniHub as Streamlined VSAT Platform
summary: SpaceBridge launched UniHub, a compact all-in-one VSAT hub platform designed to simplify satellite network deployment with high performance, efficiency, and scalability.
